Patch Set 6:
(1 comment)
There are still some issues to be fixed:
1. obdfilter-survey script doesn't clean up all obd devices at the end of test;
2. client side module reference count leakage for 'obdclass'
There are several other patches that need to be merged into this fix as well:
https://review.whamcloud.com/#/c/9350/17
https://review.whamcloud.com/#/c/23738/5
lustre/obdecho/echo.c
Line 62:
This implements echo target device. OSS provides service threads. It would be more accurate to change the name to 'echo_target_device'.

parents.subject: LU-10752 build: fix rpm packaging issues for gss

commit.subject: LU-7420 echo: fix echo server to work with unified target
commit.message:
LU-7420 echo: fix echo server to work with unified target

After Unified Target introduction the echo server lost its
ability to serve incoming request, i.e. works like fake OFD.
Patch restores that functionality, so echo server is able to
process requests from the echo client via network.

Test-Parameters: trivial testlist=obdfilter-survey
Signed-off-by: Mikhail Pershin <mike.pershin@intel.com>
Signed-off-by: Elena Gryaznova <c17455@cray.com>
